transcript:
Rep: Hey Jane, thanks for taking the time today. Really appreciate it.

Jane: Of course, happy to chat. We've been looking into solutions for a while now.

Rep: Great. So tell me a bit about what's going on at Acme Corp right now. What's driving this evaluation?

Jane: Sure. So the biggest pain point for us is our current onboarding process. It takes new customers about three weeks to get fully set up, and we're losing a lot of them during that window. Our churn in the first 90 days is around 35%, which is way too high.

Rep: Wow, 35% — that's significant. Is that something that's gotten worse recently?

Jane: Yeah, it's been creeping up. We grew our sales team last quarter so we're bringing in more customers, but our CS team hasn't scaled to match. So the handoff from sales to customer success is really messy right now.

Rep: I see. And what does that handoff look like today?

Jane: Honestly, it's mostly Slack messages and a shared Google Doc. There's no structured process. Sometimes the CS team doesn't even know a new customer is coming until they get a support ticket.

Rep: That's a common problem we hear. One thing our platform does really well is automating that handoff — we can pull in the deal context from your CRM and create a structured onboarding plan automatically. Would that help?

Jane: Absolutely. That would save us a ton of time. The other thing I'm concerned about is reporting. Right now I have no visibility into where customers are in the onboarding process. I'm basically asking my team in standup every morning.

Rep: We have a real-time dashboard that shows exactly that. I can show you a demo next week if you'd like.

Jane: That would be great. Can we do Tuesday or Wednesday?

Rep: Tuesday at 2pm work for you?

Jane: Perfect. Can you also send me a one-pager on the pricing? I need to loop in our VP of Operations, Mike Chen, before we go further.

Rep: Absolutely, I'll send that over today. And just to confirm — you're the main evaluator on this, and Mike would be the final decision maker?

Jane: That's right. Mike signs off on anything over $50K annually.

Rep: Got it. I'll make sure the pricing doc addresses ROI clearly so it's easy for Mike to evaluate. Anything else you'd like me to cover?

Jane: I think that's good for now. Looking forward to the demo.

Rep: Same here. Thanks again Jane, talk to you Tuesday!
